title: Add MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES cap and per-open slug re-check to bridge section
objective: The bridge section in bot_core.py bypasses the MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES guard entirely. During the 2026-04-04 incident, this allowed 6 concurrent processes to each open independent trades for the same game slugs, producing 2–3× duplicate positions per game and -$218 in losses. Fix: (1) skip bridge entirely if open_count is already at cap, (2) re-fetch open trades before each individual bridge open so the check reflects the true DB state within the same loop iteration.
incident_context: On 2026-04-04, 6 bot_core.py + 3 launch_all.py instances were running simultaneously. Each process fetched open_slugs before any write had landed, saw the slug as not-open, and all opened it — producing 3× positions per game. The bridge had no concurrent cap, compounding the race condition.
exact_section: bot_core.py lines 439–471 — the '# Model bridge (paper only)' block
current_code_summary: bridge block does: fetch open_trades once → get_approved_intents(open_slugs) → for each intent: open_position() + insert. No cap check anywhere. open_slugs not refreshed between opens.

required_changes.1_cap_check_before_bridge: After fetching open_trades at bridge entry, check if len(open_trades) >= MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES. If so, log 'BRIDGE SKIP — at capacity (N/M)' and skip the entire bridge block for this loop.
required_changes.2_re_fetch_per_intent: Inside the per-intent loop, call fetch_open_trades() again before each open_position(). Re-derive current_open_count and current_open_slugs from this fresh fetch. If current_open_count >= MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES, log 'BRIDGE CAP HIT' and break. If intent slug is already in current_open_slugs (race condition: another process beat us), log 'BRIDGE RACE SKIP' and continue.
required_changes.3_no_other_changes: Do not touch check_entry_gates(), model_bridge.py, or any other file. Only bot_core.py bridge section.

pseudocode: logger.info('BRIDGE SKIP — at capacity (%d/%d)', len(open_trades), MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES)
logger.info('BRIDGE CAP HIT — stopping at %d/%d', len(current_open), MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES)
if intent['slug'] in {t.market_slug for t in current_open}:
acceptance_criteria: With MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES=3 and 2 positions already open, bridge opens at most 1 position per loop
With MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES=3 and 3+ positions already open, bridge logs 'BRIDGE SKIP' and opens nothing
If a slug appears in the DB between the outer fetch and the per-intent fetch, inner check catches it and logs 'BRIDGE RACE SKIP'
Existing native signal loop (check_entry_gates) is unchanged
verification: python -m sports_bot_v2.bot_core (or cd sports_bot_v2 && python bot_core.py)
Confirm log shows 'BRIDGE DISABLED' (bridge kill switch is still False — do NOT re-enable for this task)
Code review only: confirm cap check and per-intent re-fetch are present in the bridge block
notes: ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE is currently False — do NOT change it as part of this task. This fix is a prerequisite for re-enabling.
MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES is read from env (default 3) — use the module-level constant, do not hardcode.
fetch_open_trades() is already imported — no new imports needed.
